test.command: echo 'Tests cannot run on Mac - write tests for CI only'
test.notes: Test suite does not run on macOS per services.yaml. Tests are written for CI (Linux). Same constraint as round 1.

typecheck.notes: 1765 mypy errors found in 91 files (checked 122 source files). All errors are pre-existing across the full codebase — same baseline as round 1. No new type errors introduced by v3-scrutiny-missing-tests changes.

lint.notes: ruff check exits with 148 errors (pre-existing). Same baseline as round 1. No new lint issues introduced by v3-scrutiny-missing-tests changes.

reviewsSummary.notes: Re-run reviewed only the fix feature (v3-scrutiny-missing-tests). The 3 previously-passing features (v3-inverted-index, v3-price-regression-tests, cross-area-regression-tests) already passed in round 1 and were not re-reviewed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that `py_compile` and source code inspection (using Python without brownie connection) can be used to verify structural test logic locally, even though the full test suite doesn't run on macOS. Workers should run `python -m py_compile tests/path/to/test.py` and import-check structural tests before submitting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v3-scrutiny-missing-tests worker noted in skillFeedback: 'The skill note Tests don't run on macOS is helpful, but could also mention that py_compile and source code inspection can be used to verify structural test logic locally.' Same feedback pattern seen across multiple v3-pool-index workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When creating scrutiny-fix feature descriptions, distinguish between 'confirm coverage exists' vs 'write new test'. VAL-V2CD-002 required no new test (the pre-existing test already covered it), but the feature description implied a new test was needed. This confusion causes workers to write unnecessary duplicate tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v3-scrutiny-missing-tests worker: 'VAL-V2CD-002: The test_new_pool_added_to_index_incrementally() already existed and was correct; no changes needed.' The feature description said 'Add test for new V2 pools from polling added to index', leading to potential duplicate work.

rejectedObservations.observation: VAL-V2IDX-009 structural test (test_v2_weth_special_case_decision_documented) would not catch removal of the WETH branch in pools_for_token()
rejectedObservations.reason: The companion integration test (test_v2_weth_special_case_returns_only_hardcoded_pools) would catch this regression. Together the two tests provide adequate coverage. Not a blocking issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: VAL-V2CD-004 integration test only checks the first fork via next(iter(UNISWAPS))
rejectedObservations.reason: The structural companion test (test_all_v2_forks_have_reusable_events_structural) covers all forks for is_reusable and _pool_index checks. Together they provide adequate coverage. Not a blocking issue.
